questions.q: Which of the following was a cause of the crisis of the constitutional order?
opts: (A) Rise of the middle class
(B) Rise of the working class
(C) Rise of the middle class and the working class
(D) Rise of the upper class
questions.explanation: The rise of the middle class and the working class led to the emergence of new social forces that challenged the traditional elite and the existing power structure. This led to a crisis of the constitutional order.
questions.topic: Causes of Crisis of Constitutional Order

questions.q: What was the significance of the Indian National Congress's (INC) split in 1969?
opts: (A) It led to the formation of a new nationalist party
(B) It marked the end of the nationalist movement
(C) It led to the emergence of a new leadership within the INC
(D) It led to the decline of the INC's influence
questions.explanation: The INC split in 1969 led to the emergence of a new leadership within the party, which had a significant impact on the party's policies and direction.

questions.q: Which of the following best describes the consequences of the Emergency declared by Indira Gandhi in 1975?
opts: (A) It led to the strengthening of democratic institutions
(B) It marked the beginning of a new era of democratic reforms
(C) It led to the erosion of civil liberties and the rule of law
(D) It resulted in the resignation of the Prime Minister
questions.explanation: The Emergency declared by Indira Gandhi in 1975 led to the erosion of civil liberties and the rule of law, as the government suspended fundamental rights and imposed strict controls on the media and opposition.

questions.q: Who was the President of India during the Emergency declared by Indira Gandhi in 1975?
opts: (A) Fakhruddin Ali Ahmed
(B) V.V. Giri
(C) Zakir Husain
(D) Neelam Sanjiva Reddy
questions.explanation: Fakhruddin Ali Ahmed was the President of India during the Emergency declared by Indira Gandhi in 1975.

questions.q: What was the impact of the Janata Party's government on the country's politics after the Emergency?
opts: (A) It led to a strengthening of the executive branch
(B) It marked a return to democratic norms and institutions
(C) It led to the emergence of a new nationalist party
(D) It resulted in the decline of the INC's influence
questions.explanation: The Janata Party's government after the Emergency marked a return to democratic norms and institutions, as it restored fundamental rights and lifted controls on the media and opposition.

questions.q: Which of the following best describes the significance of the 1977 elections?
opts: (A) It marked the beginning of a new era of democratic reforms
(B) It led to the strengthening of the executive branch
(C) It marked a return to democratic norms and institutions
(D) It resulted in the emergence of a new nationalist party
questions.explanation: The 1977 elections marked a return to democratic norms and institutions, as the people voted out the INC and elected the Janata Party to power.

questions.q: Who was the Prime Minister of India during the 1977 elections?
opts: (A) Morarji Desai
(B) Charan Singh
(C) Atal Bihari Vajpayee
(D) L.K. Advani
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai was the Prime Minister of India during the 1977 elections.

questions.q: What was the impact of the Mandal Commission's report on the country's politics?
opts: (A) It led to a strengthening of the executive branch
(B) It marked a return to democratic norms and institutions
(C) It led to the emergence of a new nationalist party
(D) It resulted in the erosion of civil liberties and the rule of law
questions.explanation: The Mandal Commission's report resulted in the erosion of civil liberties and the rule of law, as the government implemented policies that were seen as divisive and discriminatory.

questions.q: Which of the following best describes the consequences of the Ayodhya movement?
opts: (A) It led to a strengthening of the executive branch
(B) It marked a return to democratic norms and institutions
(C) It led to the erosion of civil liberties and the rule of law
(D) It resulted in the emergence of a new nationalist party
questions.explanation: The Ayodhya movement led to the erosion of civil liberties and the rule of law, as the government imposed strict controls on the media and opposition, and the movement resulted in communal violence and unrest.

questions.q: Who was the Prime Minister of India during the Ayodhya movement?
opts: (A) P.V. Narasimha Rao
(B) Atal Bihari Vajpayee
(C) L.K. Advani
(D) Rajiv Gandhi
questions.explanation: P.V. Narasimha Rao was the Prime Minister of India during the Ayodhya movement.
